questions.text: Can you provide a one-sentence Intent statement for this spec? For example: 'To provide a minimal, verifiable HTTP ingestion service that receives and persists audit events from agent-fox instances for integration testing purposes.'
questions.context: The Intent section is a required field in the spec format and must be a discrete, clearly articulated statement rather than prose embedded in an Overview.
options: Use the suggested statement above as-is
Use a modified version of the suggested statement
Provide a custom intent statement

questions.text: Even for an early-phase test service, can you define at least one minimal, verifiable success criterion for the Goals section?
questions.context: Without any goals, there is no basis for determining when the service is 'done' or passing. Even a simple functional goal helps downstream artifact generation.
options: The service successfully accepts a POST /events request with a valid bearer token and stores the raw event body in SQLite, returning a 2xx response.
The service passes a defined end-to-end smoke test: send one event, confirm it is persisted in the database.
No goals — this is a throwaway test harness and correctness is judged informally.

questions.text: Can you provide any additional background on the agent-fox (af) tooling and why this service is needed as a test harness?
questions.context: The Background section is explicitly marked as incomplete in the PRD. Even a few sentences about what agent-fox is, why an audit event ingestion service is needed to validate it, and whether any prior approaches were tried would satisfy this requirement.

questions.text: What should the confirmed path for the event ingestion endpoint be?
questions.context: The PRD currently states 'POST /events (or equivalent path to be confirmed during implementation)', which is ambiguous and could lead to inconsistency between the spec and the implementation.
options: POST /events
POST /api/events
POST /v1/events
To be confirmed — leave ambiguous

questions.text: What HTTP status code and response body should the POST /events endpoint return on success?
questions.context: The PRD specifies only 'an appropriate HTTP success response', leaving the exact status code and body shape undefined. This affects client integration and test assertions.
options: 201 Created with an empty body
201 Created with a JSON body containing a generated event ID
200 OK with an empty body
202 Accepted with an empty body

questions.text: Should the service perform any minimal validation on the incoming payload before storing it, beyond authentication?
questions.context: The PRD states the payload structure is unspecified and the handler should store raw JSON as received. It is unclear whether the service should reject requests with a non-JSON Content-Type or an empty body.
options: No validation — store any body regardless of Content-Type or content
Require Content-Type: application/json; reject with 400 if missing
Require Content-Type: application/json and a non-empty body; reject with 400 otherwise
To be decided during implementation

questions.text: What is the minimum SQLite schema for stored events? At minimum, should the service auto-record an ingestion timestamp alongside the raw JSON body?
questions.context: The PRD does not define the database schema. Without this, the implementation will make undocumented assumptions about column names, types, and whether metadata (e.g. received_at timestamp) is captured.
options: Single table with columns: id (auto-increment), payload (TEXT/JSON), received_at (DATETIME, auto-set on insert)
Single table with columns: id (UUID), payload (TEXT/JSON), received_at (DATETIME, auto-set on insert)
Schema is entirely left to the implementer — not part of this spec

questions.text: Who is the owner of this spec?
questions.context: The owner field is empty. An identified owner is important for accountability, review sign-off, and future spec updates.

answers.q1: Use the suggested statement above as-is
answers.q2: The service successfully accepts a POST /events request with a valid bearer token and stores the raw event body in SQLite, returning a 2xx response.
answers.q3: Running the same sequence of af and spec commands based on the same prd.md file will allow us to evaluate the performance of af and spec commands.
answers.q6: Require Content-Type: application/json and a non-empty body; reject with 400 otherwise
answers.q7: Single table with columns: id (UUID), payload (TEXT/JSON), received_at (DATETIME, auto-set on insert)
